name: Cardamom Kulfi Pops
recipeInstructions:
1. In a 6-8 quart pan over high heat, stir together the milk, sugar, and cardamom until simmering.
2. Over medium-high heat, boil the mixture until reduced to about 2 cups, 25-35 minutes, stirring often. If the milk threatens to boil over, slide the pan partially off the heat.
3. Let cool; to speed cooling, set the pan in ice water.
4. Set paper cups in a rimmed pan. Or, to make cones, cut 8 pieces of parchment or waxed paper into 7-1/2 inch squares. Fold each piece in half to make a triangle.
5. With the long edge toward you, bring one of the 45-degree angles to the top of the triangle, then roll towards the other angle. To close the hole at the bottom, start from the top and press one side onto the opposite side. Tape the cone in a few places to hold it together. Support each cone, pointed end down, in a cup slightly taller than the cone; set cups in a rimmed pan.
6. Divide the milk mixture among the cups or cones.
7. Freeze until the kulfi is thick but not hard, about 1 to 1-1/2 hours. Then, if desired, push an ice cream stick into each container.
8. Freeze until firm, about 2 more hours. To eat, peel off the paper. To store, seal the kulfi (still in cups or cones) in a large plastic bag; freeze for up to 2 weeks.
